content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 32 — SOPHIA Consensus Translation

**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Sources:** EMET (literal), LOGOS (formal), PNEUMA (dynamic) | **Base text:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 116r–118r (LC PDF pp. 230–234) | **LXX:** Codex Vaticanus, pp. 163–165

*Note: Ha'azinu is set in the Leningrad Codex in a distinctive two-column "brick-over-brick" poetic format (shirah layout) across five manuscript pages. This translation preserves the bicolon structure of the Hebrew with paired half-lines, following the manuscript's format. The poem is structured as a covenant lawsuit (rib): Exordium (1–3), Character witnesses: God and Israel contrasted (4–6), Historical prologue (7–14), Israel's apostasy (15–18), Divine judgment (19–33), Reversal and vindication (34–43).*

---

**1**
Give ear, O heavens, and I will speak,
    and let the earth hear the words of my mouth.

**2**
Let my teaching [liqchi] drop as the rain,
    let my speech distill as the dew,
like gentle showers upon the tender grass,
    and like heavy drops upon the herbage.

**3**
For I will proclaim the name of the LORD —
    ascribe greatness to our God!

**4**
The Rock [ha-tsur] — his work is perfect,
    for all his ways are justice.
A God of faithfulness, without iniquity —
    righteous and upright is he.

**5**
They have dealt corruptly with him;
    they are no longer his children because of their blemish —
a crooked and twisted generation.

**6**
Do you thus repay the LORD,
    O foolish and unwise people?
Is he not your Father, who created you [qonekha],
    who made you and established you?

**7**
Remember the days of old;
    consider the years of many generations.
Ask your father and he will declare to you,
    your elders and they will tell you.

**8**
When the Most High [Elyon] gave an inheritance to the nations,
    when he divided mankind,
he fixed the borders of the peoples
    according to the number of the sons of Israel.

**9**
For the LORD's portion is his people,
    Jacob his allotted heritage.

**10**
He found him in a desert land,
    and in the howling waste of the wilderness.
He encircled him, he cared for him,
    he kept him as the apple of his eye.

**11**
Like an eagle that stirs up its nest,
    that hovers over its young,
spreading out its wings, catching them,
    bearing them on its pinions —

**12**
the LORD alone led him;
    no foreign god was with him.

**13**
He made him ride on the heights of the land
    and he fed him the produce of the field,
and he suckled him with honey from the rock
    and oil from the flinty crag,

**14**
curds of cattle and milk of flocks,
    together with fat of lambs,
and rams of Bashan and goats,
    with the fat of kidneys of wheat —
and you drank the blood of the grape as foaming wine.

**15**
But Jeshurun [Yeshurun] grew fat and kicked —
    you grew fat, thick, and sleek —
and he forsook God who made him
    and scoffed at the Rock of his salvation.

**16**
They stirred his jealousy with foreign gods;
    with abominations [to'evot] they provoked him to anger.

**17**
They sacrificed to demons [shedim] that were no gods,
    to gods they had not known,
new gods that had come recently,
    whom your fathers had not dreaded.

**18**
The Rock who fathered you — you neglected,
    and you forgot the God who gave you birth.

**19**
The LORD saw it and rejected them
    because of the provocation of his sons and daughters.

**20**
And he said: "I will hide my face from them;
    I will see what their end will be,
for they are a perverse generation —
    children in whom there is no faithfulness.

**21**
They have made me jealous with what is no god;
    they have provoked me to anger with their idols [havaleihem].
So I will make them jealous with those who are no people;
    I will provoke them to anger with a foolish nation.

**22**
For a fire is kindled by my anger
    and it burns to the depths of Sheol,
and it devours the earth and its produce
    and sets ablaze the foundations of the mountains.

**23**
I will heap disasters upon them;
    my arrows I will spend against them.

**24**
Wasting of hunger and consumed by burning fever
    and bitter plague;
and the tooth of beasts I will send against them,
    with venom of things that crawl in the dust.

**25**
Outside, the sword shall bereave,
    and inside, terror —
for the young man and for the young woman,
    the nursing child with the man of gray hairs.

**26**
I would have said: 'I will scatter them far and wide;
    I will wipe out the memory of them from among men' —

**27**
had I not feared the taunt of the enemy,
    lest their adversaries misunderstand,
lest they say: 'Our hand has triumphed,
    and the LORD has not done all this.'

**28**
For they are a nation void of counsel,
    and there is no understanding in them.

**29**
If only they were wise, they would perceive this;
    they would understand their latter end.

**30**
How could one chase a thousand
    and two put ten thousand to flight,
unless their Rock had sold them
    and the LORD had given them up?

**31**
For their rock is not as our Rock —
    even our enemies themselves are the judges.

**32**
For their vine is from the vine of Sodom
    and from the fields of Gomorrah;
their grapes are grapes of poison;
    bitter clusters are theirs.

**33**
Their wine is the venom of serpents
    and the cruel poison of asps.

**34**
Is this not stored up with me,
    sealed in my treasuries?

**35**
Vengeance is mine, and recompense,
    for the time when their foot shall slip;
for the day of their calamity is at hand,
    and their doom comes swiftly upon them.

**36**
For the LORD will vindicate his people
    and have compassion on his servants,
when he sees that their strength is gone
    and there is none remaining, bond or free.

**37**
And he will say: 'Where are their gods,
    the rock in which they took refuge,

**38**
who ate the fat of their sacrifices
    and drank the wine of their drink offerings?
Let them rise up and help you;
    let them be your protection!

**39**
See now that I, even I, am he,
    and there is no god beside me.
I kill and I make alive;
    I wound and I myself heal —
and there is none who can deliver from my hand.

**40**
For I lift my hand to the heavens
    and swear: As I live forever,

**41**
if I sharpen my flashing sword
    and my hand takes hold of judgment,
I will take vengeance on my adversaries
    and will repay those who hate me.

**42**
I will make my arrows drunk with blood,
    and my sword shall devour flesh —
with the blood of the slain and the captive,
    from the long-haired heads of the enemy.'

**43**
Rejoice, O nations, with his people,
    for he avenges the blood of his servants,
and takes vengeance on his adversaries
    and makes atonement for his land and his people.

---

**44** And Moses came and recited all the words of this song in the hearing of the people, he and Hoshea son of Nun. **45** And when Moses had finished speaking all these words to all Israel, **46** he said to them: "Set your heart to all the words with which I am testifying among you today, which you shall command your children to take care to do — all the words of this instruction [torah]. **47** For it is no empty thing for you, but it is your life, and by this word you shall live long in the land that you are crossing the Jordan to possess."

**48** And the LORD spoke to Moses that same day, saying: **49** "Go up to this mountain of the Abarim, Mount Nebo, which is in the land of Moab, opposite Jericho, and view the land of Canaan, which I am giving to the sons of Israel for a possession. **50** And die on the mountain that you go up, and be gathered to your people, as Aaron your brother died on Mount Hor and was gathered to his people, **51** because you broke faith with me in the midst of the sons of Israel at the waters of Meribath-Kadesh, in the wilderness of Zin, and because you did not treat me as holy in the midst of the sons of Israel. **52** For you shall see the land before you, but you shall not go there — into the land that I am giving to the sons of Israel."

---

## SOPHIA's Council Notes

**Textual base — MT vs. LXX:** The Leningrad Codex MT is the project's base text. Several major LXX divergences are noted in the disagreement log. The most significant are V.8 (sons of Israel vs. sons of God/divine beings) and V.43 (shorter MT vs. expanded LXX/DSS text). SOPHIA follows the MT for both.

**V.5 — Crux interpretum:** שִׁחֵת לוֹ לֹא בָּנָיו (shichet lo lo vanav) is among the most difficult phrases in the Torah. EMET's literal rendering is preserved. The LXX's substantially different text is a parallel tradition, not a superior reading.

**V.8 — "sons of Israel" (MT):** Despite PNEUMA's argument for the LXX/DSS "sons of God" reading, SOPHIA follows the project's commitment to the MT Leningrad Codex as base text. The variant is noted in the disagreement log. LOGOS's note that this is "one of the most important variants in Deuteronomy" is well-taken; the MT is followed with full awareness of the alternative.

**V.15 — Yeshurun:** The poetic name is preserved with bracketed transliteration. The irony of "the upright one" growing fat and kicking is the rhetorical heart of the strophe and must not be lost by substituting "Israel."

**V.17 — shedim:** "Demons [shedim]" is retained with the bracketed Hebrew gloss. These are not merely "nothing" — they are real powers in the biblical worldview, just not the true God.

**V.21 — havaleihem:** "Their idols [havaleihem]" — havel (breath/vanity/emptiness) is rendered as "idols" in context, with the gloss preserving the Hebrew resonance. LOGOS's "idols" is adopted over EMET's "vanities."

**V.35 — "Vengeance is mine":** The celebrated verse is rendered following the MT's direct statement. The LXX paraphrases this verse significantly. Paul's citation in Romans 12:19 follows a text closer to the MT.

**V.44 — "Hoshea":** The MT uses Hoshea (Joshua's earlier name) at this verse — a deliberate and meaningful textual choice. EMET preserves it; LOGOS and PNEUMA replace with "Joshua." SOPHIA follows the MT and uses Hoshea.

**V.43 — MT text:** The shorter MT form of v.43 is followed, not the expanded LXX/DSS version. The LXX addition ("let all the sons of God worship him," cited in Hebrews 1:6) is a significant theological expansion but is not the MT. PNEUMA notes following "the fuller text" — SOPHIA does not.

— *SOPHIA*
